column4: Questions
[XYZ] the correct URL?
What are the responsibilities?
Is there an existing JD?
Hours/wk or range? 20 is the minimum and they *have* to pay for it whether they use them or not
I envision a role like this running $X-$Y. Does that sound OK?
Have you budgeted for this role? (If startup, see inserted note)
Are there any certifications needed?
Is an accent ok or do they need to sound like they're from the U.S.?
What kind of growth projectory can they see for this role?

Have you engaged anyone else or are you yourselves trying to fill this role?
For Quilt - is your internal team recruiting for this role? If so, how long before you expect to find someone?
Are they hiring for more than 1 role?

Have hiring manager, CEO and the CFO committed to this role? 
I'll need to speak with the person IN the company closest to this hire.
Who's the manager and when can I speak with them? I need a deeper understanding of the role
Have they ever worked with someone like this (remote) before? +
Are there any qualities that are especially important to have or to avoid?
What tools or software are used in the role?
Are they clear on the metrics this person would have?
Who would this role report to?
Any notion as to whether and when the hours may increase?
What personality are they looking for? How would you describe your culture?
Will you meet a candidate w/i 24-48 hours of when we introduce them to you?
Someone else they should meet? Possible to meet within 48 hours?
Our MO is that within 48 hours of the interview, a decision is made and there is sign-off (explain A-players move fast). Does that sound feasible for you?
As far as how our pricing works, it's very simple, you pay by hour for the time you use. You pay us and we pay the person
This is a permanent arrangemnet
We'll ask about the possibility of a raise for the staffer at the 1-year mark.
Prompt for SDRs: would you consider $5/hr more for an interstellar candidate?
Are they willing to pay $250 if they cancel the order?
If they've been a "bad client" in the past, we'll charge a fee of $1,000 up front
Talking points
Clarify what they can expect from us and what we expect from them
Let me speak with our recruiting team to confirm when we can plan to have a candidate for you
[If we're at capacity] Announce there's a wait period before getting their order started.
We require clients to onboard our staffers using this checklist. We'll guide and help them through it.
Take client through the presentation (Prezi, client deck)
Highlight  the benefits of oversee staff more - no paid holidays, no overhead, no legal, or administrative mess [California clients love us]
Josh to-fill post intake
Loom link
Key requirements for the role
# test score needed
Spoken English level required
Written English level required
Assessment for the role
Rate client confirmed
Executive Assistant
Emails: Zero inbox. Screen. Respond. Draft
Set up Meetings/Optimize calendar
Facilitate meetings
Take meeting minutes
Bill pay - processing bills
Support your leadership team or Liaise between you and team members
Phone: screen calls, make calls
Social media management
Project Mgmt: vendors, clients, internal
Presentations: create, graphs, collate info
Spreadsheets: gather data, format
Creating basic reports.
Travel: Book travel/make other reservations
Doc Mgmt: Expense reports, invoicing, intranet
Data Entry: CRM, ERP, lists, surveys
Research
Customer service
Email blasts
Shopping: researching lowest price for softwares, office supplies, gifts.
Outreach Specialist
Lead Generation Expert
Online Community Manager
Podcast Management
Tradeshows
Personal Matters (paying caregivers, setting up appointments etc)
Transcription (transcribing voicemail, video or audio, podcasts etc.)
Project Manager
What type of projects will the PM manage?
What stage of the project is the PM coming in at?
Sales
Is any geographic location relevant?
